title: Chop Suey Chop-Chop Day!
story: Every August 29, food lovers celebrate National Chop Suey Day by digging into this tasty stir-fried dish! Chop suey is packed with vegetables, meat, and a savory sauce served over rice or noodles. Many families visit their favorite Chinese restaurants or try cooking it at home. The name means "mixed pieces" in Chinese, which makes perfect sense when you see all the colorful ingredients tossed together in one delicious bowl!
activity_question: What does the name "chop suey" mean in Chinese?
bonus_fact: Chop suey was actually invented in America, not China!

title: Pucker Up for National Lemon Juice Day!
story: Every August 29, people celebrate National Lemon Juice Day by squeezing fresh lemons and enjoying their tangy goodness! Some brave souls drink pure lemon juice and make hilarious sour faces, while others mix it into lemonade, salad dressings, or yummy desserts. Restaurants offer special lemon-flavored treats, and social media fills with videos of people trying the ultimate pucker challenge. It's the perfect day to appreciate this super sour citrus that adds zing to our favorite foods and drinks!
activity_question: What do some people do with pure lemon juice on this holiday?
activity_choices: Drink it and make sour faces
activity_answer: Drink it and make sour faces
bonus_fact: One lemon contains more vitamin C than most people need in a whole day!

title: Spice Up Your Life Day!
story: Chefs and home cooks everywhere celebrate More Herbs, Less Salt Day on August 29 by swapping boring salt for amazing herbs and spices! People experiment with basil, oregano, garlic powder, and cinnamon to make their food taste incredible without all the sodium. It's a delicious way to stay healthy!
activity_question: What do people use instead of salt on this holiday?
bonus_fact: Fresh herbs can have up to 10 times more flavor than dried ones!

title: India's Amazing Athletic Adventure Day
story: Every August 29, millions of kids and adults across India lace up their sneakers for National Sports Day! This special day celebrates the birthday of hockey legend Major Dhyan Chand, who helped India win three Olympic gold medals. Schools organize fun runs, cricket matches, and kabaddi tournaments. Sports clubs host free coaching sessions where kids can try new games like badminton or table tennis. The government gives out awards to amazing athletes, and families gather to play traditional games together. It's like the whole country becomes one giant playground for the day!
activity_question: What sport was Major Dhyan Chand famous for playing?
bonus_fact: Major Dhyan Chand was so good that people called him the 'Wizard of Hockey'!
